ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'meat pie' class (FOODON_03315595); no specific entry for Men Ding Rou Bing (Beijing doornail pork pie). Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers meat pies and pasties broadly; no specific code for this traditional Beijing pan-fried pork pastry exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for Men Ding Rou Bing or equivalent traditional Chinese pork pastry.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Men Ding Rou Bing is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one medium-sized pan-fried pork meat pie as typically served at a Beijing snack stall or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: 门钉肉饼 / Pork Meat Pie — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 门钉肉饼 (Pork Meat Pie), cr_id G00010148. Carbohydrate is recorded as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source, which likely reflects the pork filling component only; the wheat flour wrapper contributes additional carbohydrate not captured in this entry. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported in the HPB source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Men Ding Rou Bing (Beijing doornail pork pie). GI estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with wheat-flour-based pan-fried dumplings and meat pastries from the University of Sydney GI Database. The recorded carbohydrate for this HPB entry is 0.0 g/100g (reflecting the pork filling only); however, the wheat flour wrapper is a known component of this dish and contributes meaningful carbohydrate. Glycemic load per 100 g serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g) / 100 = (55 × 0.0) / 100 = 0.0 based on the HPB-recorded carbohydrate value; the true GL is likely higher given the wheat flour wrapper. This limitation is documented in notes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold; potassium at 47 mg/100 g provides negligible offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Men Ding Rou Bing due to its very high sodium content of 1050 mg/100 g, which substantially exceeds safe limits; if consumed, portion size should be minimised and no additional salty condiments used.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Wheat flour wrapper contributes refined carbohydrate and raises glycaemic load despite 0 g/100 g recorded carbohydrate in the HPB entry
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with Men Ding Rou Bing because the wheat flour pastry wrapper contributes significant refined carbohydrate not captured in the HPB nutrient record, raising the effective glycaemic load; portion control and pairing with non-starchy vegetables is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100 g), cholesterol (89 mg/100 g), and pan-frying preparation increase cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of Men Ding Rou Bing due to its high sodium content, moderate cholesterol, and pan-fried preparation which adds dietary fat; the combination of these factors elevates blood pressure and atherogenic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork (high arachidonic acid / omega-6), refined wheat flour wrapper, and pan-frying are all pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Men Ding Rou Bing as pork is rich in arachidonic acid, the refined wheat flour wrapper raises glycaemic load, and pan-frying adds oxidised fats — all of which amplify inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Men Ding Rou Bing (门钉肉饼), literally 'doornail meat pie', is a traditional Beijing street snack with a history spanning several centuries. The dish consists of a seasoned pork mince filling — typically mixed with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and spring onion — encased in a soft wheat flour dough wrapper that is pan-fried until the exterior develops a golden, crispy crust while the interior remains juicy and tender. The name derives from the shape of ancient decorative doornails (门钉) found on imperial palace gates, and the dish is considered auspicious, symbolising good fortune and wealth. In Singapore, it is served as a savoury snack or light meal at Chinese restaurants and food courts. Nutritionally, the HPB-recorded profile shows 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, and 10.5 g fat per 100 g, with a notably high sodium content of 1050 mg/100 g from soy sauce seasoning. The recorded carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100 g likely reflects the pork filling component only and does not capture the wheat flour wrapper contribution. Clinically, the dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension due to very high sodium, and requires caution for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to the refined carbohydrate in the pastry wrapper. The pan-fried preparation and pork content also make it pro-inflammatory and unsuitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients. Contains gluten (wheat) allergen.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010148) with confidence 0.95. The HPB entry is an exact match for this dish. The only limitation is that the recorded carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100 g likely reflects the pork filling only and does not capture the wheat flour wrapper; this is documented in notes.
notes: carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database entry G00010148 for the recorded pork filling component; however, this value likely reflects the pork filling component only and does not capture the carbohydrate contributed by the wheat flour dough wrapper — the true whole-dish carbohydrate is expected to be substantially higher. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish as of 2026-04-16. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as 0.0 based on the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0.0 g/100 g; the true GL is likely moderate-to-high given the wheat flour wrapper. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour pan-fried meat pastries from the University of Sydney GI Database — no direct GI measurement for Men Ding Rou Bing exists in any approved source.
